# Support

Thanks for using `commitment-issues`! Here is how to get help.

## Before you ask

Most questions are already answered in the docs:

- [README](../README.md) — quickstart, what it catches, and how commit/push
  checks behave.
- [FAQ](../docs/faq.md) — adoption, safety, configuration, test runners, package
  managers, CI, and removal.
- [Configuration and Behavior](../docs/configuration.md) — full configuration
  reference and internals.
- [Message states](../docs/message-states.md) — a gallery of the output states
  you might see.

If hooks stopped running, try `npm run doctor` first — it verifies and repairs
hook wiring.

## Asking a question

- **Usage questions and how-tos:** open an issue using the **Question** option in
  the [issue tracker](https://github.com/RoryGlenn/commitment-issues/issues/new/choose).
- **Bugs:** open a **Bug report** with reproduction steps.
- **Feature ideas:** open a **Feature request** describing the problem you want
  to solve.

Please search [existing issues](https://github.com/RoryGlenn/commitment-issues/issues?q=is%3Aissue)
first — someone may have already asked.

## Reporting security issues

Do **not** open a public issue for security vulnerabilities. Follow the process
in [SECURITY.md](SECURITY.md) instead.

## Response expectations

This is an open-source project maintained on a best-effort basis. We appreciate
your patience, and well-scoped issues with clear reproduction steps get the
fastest responses.
